--- Thread (29 messages, 2018-04-15) ---
[2018-04-15 06:22] .bky_1556 (bky): Is simultaneous Redeemer+Antima worth it, or is it generally better to hold off on Redeemer for a bigger Antima?
[2018-04-15 06:23] .bky_1556 (bky): Assuming double B is a sunk cost for an absorber.
[2018-04-15 06:24] Deleted User (Deleted User): it depends how high the inflation is
[2018-04-15 06:24] Deleted User (Deleted User): higher the inflation more u want to deemer I think
[2018-04-15 06:24] Deleted User (Deleted User): lower the inflation??
[2018-04-15 06:24] .bky_1556 (bky): The nice thing about Redeemer+Antima is that the Antima engineers arrive just in time to soak the Redeemer charges.
[2018-04-15 06:25] .bky_1556 (bky): So the combo is relatively flat to inflation.
[2018-04-15 06:25] .bky_1556 (bky): Too late, though, and you won't be able to engi up enough to make the antima viable.
[2018-04-15 06:25] Deleted User (Deleted User): yes but you gain more engineers in one case and i'm not sure what inflation you'd rather have the engineers in
[2018-04-15 06:25] Deleted User (Deleted User): probs rather deemer in low inflation yeah
[2018-04-15 06:26] .bky_1556 (bky): It works out to {Redeemer + 1 dmg) vs. (5 engineers + a G)
[2018-04-15 06:26] .bky_1556 (bky): 3 of the Redeemer dmg is frontloaded by a turn.
[2018-04-15 06:27] .bky_1556 (bky): I think the Redeemer comes out ahead at all inflations.
[2018-04-15 06:27] .bky_1556 (bky): That assumes the Redeemer isn't giving them absorb they wouldn't otherwise have for the intervening turn, though.
[2018-04-15 06:30] Deleted User (Deleted User): wait comes out ahead?????
[2018-04-15 06:30] Deleted User (Deleted User): it feels like there's an inflation where 5 engineers is better
[2018-04-15 06:31] Deleted User (Deleted User): this is the turn u buy antima right
[2018-04-15 06:31] Deleted User (Deleted User): ok sure I can believe that
[2018-04-15 06:36] .bky_1556 (bky): Yes, you buy the redeemer and antima simultaneously.
[2018-04-15 06:37] .bky_1556 (bky): Hmm, actually my analysis seems empirically wrong when inflation increases sharply in the intervening turn.
[2018-04-15 06:38] .bky_1556 (bky): Ah, I see it. I'm undervaluing the incoming gauss charges.
[2018-04-15 06:39] .bky_1556 (bky): In practice, the relevant comparison is actually (mature Redeemer + 1 dmg) vs. (11 engineers + a G)
[2018-04-15 06:39] .bky_1556 (bky): mature = gauss charges handled.
[2018-04-15 06:41] .bky_1556 (bky): This puts the Redeemer at a moderate deficit on paper.
[2018-04-15 06:42] .bky_1556 (bky): In practice, the surplus engineers would likely be overdefense for a few turns, so it's closer.
[2018-04-15 06:43] .bky_1556 (bky): But inflation would strongly favor the engineers, not because the math does, but because it means they're less likely to overdefend for 2+ more turns.
[2018-04-15 06:51] .bky_1556 (bky): ...except if the engineers were overdefending for 3 turns, you would just buy the redeemer when the comet goes off.
[2018-04-15 06:52] .bky_1556 (bky): Verdict: Cute maneuver, but probably not worth it unless you're launching a maximum comet anyway.
[2018-04-15 06:56] Deleted User (Deleted User): i'm too tired to understand

messages.author: .bky_1556
messages.content: Is simultaneous Redeemer+Antima worth it, or is it generally better to hold off on Redeemer for a bigger Antima?

messages.author: .bky_1556
messages.content: Assuming double B is a sunk cost for an absorber.

messages.author: Deleted User
messages.content: it depends how high the inflation is

messages.author: Deleted User
messages.content: higher the inflation more u want to deemer I think

messages.author: Deleted User
messages.content: lower the inflation??

messages.author: .bky_1556
messages.content: The nice thing about Redeemer+Antima is that the Antima engineers arrive just in time to soak the Redeemer charges.

messages.author: .bky_1556
messages.content: So the combo is relatively flat to inflation.

messages.author: .bky_1556
messages.content: Too late, though, and you won't be able to engi up enough to make the antima viable.

messages.author: Deleted User
messages.content: yes but you gain more engineers in one case and i'm not sure what inflation you'd rather have the engineers in

messages.author: Deleted User
messages.content: probs rather deemer in low inflation yeah

messages.author: .bky_1556
messages.content: It works out to {Redeemer + 1 dmg) vs. (5 engineers + a G)

messages.author: .bky_1556
messages.content: 3 of the Redeemer dmg is frontloaded by a turn.

messages.author: .bky_1556
messages.content: I think the Redeemer comes out ahead at all inflations.

messages.author: .bky_1556
messages.content: That assumes the Redeemer isn't giving them absorb they wouldn't otherwise have for the intervening turn, though.

messages.author: Deleted User
messages.content: wait comes out ahead?????

messages.author: Deleted User
messages.content: it feels like there's an inflation where 5 engineers is better

messages.author: Deleted User
messages.content: this is the turn u buy antima right

messages.author: Deleted User
messages.content: ok sure I can believe that

messages.author: .bky_1556
messages.content: Yes, you buy the redeemer and antima simultaneously.

messages.author: .bky_1556
messages.content: Hmm, actually my analysis seems empirically wrong when inflation increases sharply in the intervening turn.

messages.author: .bky_1556
messages.content: Ah, I see it. I'm undervaluing the incoming gauss charges.

messages.author: .bky_1556
messages.content: In practice, the relevant comparison is actually (mature Redeemer + 1 dmg) vs. (11 engineers + a G)

messages.author: .bky_1556
messages.content: mature = gauss charges handled.

messages.author: .bky_1556
messages.content: This puts the Redeemer at a moderate deficit on paper.

messages.author: .bky_1556
messages.content: In practice, the surplus engineers would likely be overdefense for a few turns, so it's closer.

messages.author: .bky_1556
messages.content: But inflation would strongly favor the engineers, not because the math does, but because it means they're less likely to overdefend for 2+ more turns.

messages.author: .bky_1556
messages.content: ...except if the engineers were overdefending for 3 turns, you would just buy the redeemer when the comet goes off.

messages.author: .bky_1556
messages.content: Verdict: Cute maneuver, but probably not worth it unless you're launching a maximum comet anyway.

messages.author: Deleted User
messages.content: i'm too tired to understand

[2018-04-15 12:24] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): But Auride was an "oh shit" play when I saw you were ahead 2000 Drones

[2018-04-15 12:27] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): But disregarding Fuge, p1 has no good line to get first Fab anyway in Drone+Conduit
[2018-04-15 12:27] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Then I could've done dd cc

[2018-04-15 12:28] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Yea I should've just done that
[2018-04-15 12:28] itsotter: if you had, did i have any options
[2018-04-15 12:28] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Idk, p2 has the worse new Fuge lines
[2018-04-15 12:28] itsotter: these gauss fab races seem very strange to me
[2018-04-15 12:28] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Cos you float 2 with it
[2018-04-15 12:28] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): p1 can get it on t2 without float
[2018-04-15 12:29] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): But I wouldn't say that comfortable Fab first player instawins
[2018-04-15 12:29] itsotter: uh, maybe p2 wants dd, ecc, ddd, fab? as a response to p1 doing the clean one
[2018-04-15 12:30] itsotter: you end up the half-turn behind on fab but with two more drones
[2018-04-15 12:30] itsotter: still looks bad to me
[2018-04-15 12:30] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Yea generally 2 Drones is the compensation for being behind half a turn on initiative
[2018-04-15 12:30] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): So that sounds about right
[2018-04-15 12:31] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Since we were spamming Fissions (which are not bp), those 2 Drones aren't as bad as they would've been if we were spamming Geese (which are bp)
[2018-04-15 12:32] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): But I prolly wouldn't have spammed more than a few Fissions if I didn't have a bad opening
[2018-04-15 12:32] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): But with my opening I just had no other option but to make Fissions
[2018-04-15 12:33] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): I'd make Fissions and not make 2nd Forge
[2018-04-15 12:33] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): And once I can't defend with Wall + Forcefield I'd just go bp
[2018-04-15 12:33] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): And so if I only had 1 or 2 Fissions they'd be dead from lifespan at that point anyway
[2018-04-15 12:33] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Idk if this is the correct play of playing it, but it's how I would've wanted to play it
[2018-04-15 12:35] itsotter: i guess if you're p1 you can kind of faceroll after the fabs and if you're p2 you look for something you can do with your extra income that he can't
[2018-04-15 12:36] itsotter: and hope the set has something like that, because otherwise you're going to bp town pretty soon and he's gonna shoot first

[2018-04-15 12:41] mqp: but seems reasonable

[2018-04-15 20:26] liadahlia (Lia): You're at a natural disadvantage as p1 but keep in mind that prismata is as much execution as strategy, meaning even if you're doing a mirror strat, you can still win simply because you are doing it more efficiently
[2018-04-15 20:27] liadahlia (Lia): P2 only have about 52% winrate after all, which is less than a lot of games
[2018-04-15 20:28] 307th: in most sets gauss fab is clunky enough that if you're player 1 you can just do something annoying and smorcy and mess the gauss fab player up
[2018-04-15 20:28] 307th: and gauss fab is good value, but it's not insane, so as long as you can get some kind of edge over them taking advantage of their clunky opening, you can outvalue them
[2018-04-15 20:29] liadahlia (Lia): Yes, i've won fab mirror and pre-nerfed Redeemer mirror as p1 before, it's certainly doable
[2018-04-15 20:30] 307th: poor redeemer, i really don't think it needed the nerf 😦
[2018-04-15 20:31] 307th: although it did have a really high winrate as p2
[2018-04-15 20:31] liadahlia (Lia): Anyways, fighting games have it much worse as far as winrate disparity
[2018-04-15 20:32] 307th: well, the 52% winrate is overall, it's pretty safe to say that in some sets the disparity will be much larger
[2018-04-15 20:33] liadahlia (Lia): Yes, Redeemer mirror was 58% p2?
[2018-04-15 20:33] Deleted User (Deleted User): Yeah it's misleading, the true p1 and p2 adv is generally quite a bit bigger, but they cancel each other out
[2018-04-15 20:33] liadahlia (Lia): But that is about as extreme as it gets
[2018-04-15 20:33] Deleted User (Deleted User): if it's a true redeemer set, it probs was like 65%+ but most sets aren't true redeemer sets
[2018-04-15 20:34] liadahlia (Lia): Stay away from base +5 folks
[2018-04-15 20:35] liadahlia (Lia): Idk, these winrate stats often feel much more oppressive than they really are, which is why i want people to stop being caught in a trap of thinking about winrate too much
[2018-04-15 20:36] 307th: yeah there's a big difference between 65% and 100%
[2018-04-15 20:36] wilson0862 (Wilson): About 35% difference. 🤔
[2018-04-15 20:37] 307th: player favoured sets are pretty rare, and when they happen they're almost always winnable for the disadvantaged player
[2018-04-15 20:41] liadahlia (Lia): @307th you should write a prismata library article on winrates, i see a lot of new players struggle with understanding that it doesn't matter as much as they'd think
[2018-04-15 20:42] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): What if Redeemer was 5GGB, 10 bt3 GauCha?
[2018-04-15 20:43] 307th: that'd be about on par with the current 10GB redeemer i think
[2018-04-15 20:43] liadahlia (Lia): The problem with Redeemer wasn't that it was too strong, it was that it was too strong for player 2 because there's a very natural line to rush out redeemer
[2018-04-15 20:43] 307th: probably a bit weaker, since it'd be so hard to defend the 10 charges efficiently
[2018-04-15 20:43] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): Took me a while to realize that the p1 vs p2 autowin/favorite argument is mostly exageration from some people. People keep bringing it up, so it's still annoying.
[2018-04-15 20:43] liadahlia (Lia): If you nerf it like that p1 will never get it
[2018-04-15 20:43] wilson0862 (Wilson): People have to blame things other than themselves.
[2018-04-15 20:43] 307th: yeah people just throw it out there pretty casually, lol
[2018-04-15 20:44] wilson0862 (Wilson): No RNG, so you naturally have to find something else.
[2018-04-15 20:44] Deleted User (Deleted User): if you're p1 and other kid doesn't misplay
[2018-04-15 20:44] Deleted User (Deleted User): how can u actually win
[2018-04-15 20:44] f300xen (f0x): when in doubt, blame natural conduit
[2018-04-15 20:44] wilson0862 (Wilson): Right on cue, cooledcannon.
[2018-04-15 20:45] wilson0862 (Wilson): I'm really curious what my redeemer winrates were.
[2018-04-15 20:45] wilson0862 (Wilson): I didn't mind being p1 at all, most of the time.
[2018-04-15 20:46] liadahlia (Lia): "other kid doesn't misplay" i think you're playing against the greatest player in the world
[2018-04-15 20:47] wilson0862 (Wilson): Most claims are so handwavey and vague I don't feel like they're worth addressing.
[2018-04-15 20:48] wilson0862 (Wilson): I realize what I just said might also fall in that category. :p

messages.author: Deleted User
messages.content: I don't know if you've already done it, but what about punf's golden rule(always gambit)

messages.author: 307th
messages.content: i think i touched on gambits in the chill series

messages.author: 307th
messages.content: 'always gambit' seems like a terrible rule

messages.author: Deleted User
messages.content: it's a pretty brilliant rule

messages.author: Deleted User
messages.content: i've upped my winrate/rating applying that rule

messages.author: Deleted User
messages.content: the idea is the other kid doesn't want to sac his defender/luci either

messages.author: Deleted User
messages.content: so you can play as if he won't(roughly speaking)

messages.author: 307th
messages.content: rules like that can be good as a way of saying 'here's something you probably aren't doing enough, so try doing it more'

messages.author: liadahlia
messages.content: i think rather than "always gambit" the better rule is "gambit more aggressively"

messages.author: liadahlia
messages.content: because most players don't do it at all

messages.author: Deleted User
messages.content: I feel a lot of kids think of defending as the default and look to gambit, but it seems more proper to dot he reverse

messages.author: liadahlia
messages.content: i think it's a matter of preference

messages.author: 307th
messages.content: they're difficult to do for the general population though

messages.author: 307th
messages.content: like, if someone's <1700, it's probably bad to encourage them to gambit more

messages.author: 307th
messages.content: it'd be better for them to spend their mental energy elsewhere

messages.author: Deleted User
messages.content: ah I guess cause mistakes are so devastating?

messages.author: liadahlia
messages.content: yeah and also <1700 it's hard to know what a gambit even look like

messages.author: 307th
messages.content: yeah the tactics are complicated enough that in 45s i can easily see someone burning their timebank just figuring out that there is a gambit

messages.author: liadahlia
messages.content: and good gambits look at outcomes, and it's hard for a newer player to fully visualize the outcomes

messages.author: Deleted User
messages.content: well the simplest idea is basically wall/rhino

messages.author: Deleted User
messages.content: you should just play as if he will lose the rhino

messages.author: Deleted User
messages.content: because it's bad to sorb on it(at least, even if it's proper, you should gambit anyway)

messages.author: liadahlia
messages.content: i think that's reasonable, although if you gambit and lose a dgrid it might be a bad idea to

messages.author: Deleted User
messages.content: well here's the thing you can extrapolate that to higher sorbers too I think

messages.author: liadahlia
messages.content: (and opponent doens't have a dgrid)

messages.author: Deleted User
messages.content: it's even worse if opp is saccing d grid for them

messages.author: Deleted User
messages.content: like, they certainly aren't saccing d grid to pop your d grid

messages.author: 307th
messages.content: if you're assuming they're losing xeno guardian, though, then they could definitely lose dgrid, get 3 absorb on xeno, and kill your dgrid

messages.author: 307th
messages.content: gambiting is sometimes right, and sometimes wrong

messages.author: Deleted User
messages.content: yeah i guess u don't gambit there unless it's high inflation sure

messages.author: Deleted User
messages.content: idk I love flippantly saying punf rules

messages.author: p0lari
messages.content: did you play this week's event mode

messages.author: liadahlia
messages.content: "gambit when, if your opponent takes it, result in a better position for yourself"

messages.author: p0lari
messages.content: you basically had to gambit every turn

messages.author: p0lari
messages.content: then about half my opponents actually took the gambits and about half of them were correct to do so and won with it

messages.author: Deleted User
messages.content: that reminds me of the gambit copypasta

messages.author: Deleted User
messages.content: >My favorite situation in Prismata is when one player makes a gambit, which causes a chain reaction of accepting and offering gambits. Neither player wants to defend because it is awkward for the other player to attack, and both players keep attacking because the other is not fully defending.

[2018-04-16 16:45] hologoddessofgrainandapples (Pblur): I'm pretty new, and I've lost several games to a pattern:
 * I see good blue defense in set
 * I go high econ, and get good defenses and absorb
 * I can't figure out where to go for damage, and so lose to attrition.
[2018-04-16 16:46] hologoddessofgrainandapples (Pblur): If you've got high econ and blue, plus good blue defense options, what offense should you build?
[2018-04-16 16:46] hologoddessofgrainandapples (Pblur): Is there any base-set offense that works well?
[2018-04-16 16:47] masn6811 (Masne98888): find the best damage in the set 😛
[2018-04-16 16:47] medar: Tarsier, but most of the time there is something better in the random set
[2018-04-16 16:47] masn6811 (Masne98888): if you have any replays to share, that could help
[2018-04-16 16:47] hologoddessofgrainandapples (Pblur): Not at the moment, I'm away from my prismata computer.
[2018-04-16 16:48] hologoddessofgrainandapples (Pblur): So Tarsiers are the best base-set 'I have all the defense in the world' build? Good to know.
[2018-04-16 16:48] darkeisbein: Gauss cannons and splitters are alright attackers if you are floating the reasources and have no better random set attacker to purchase, Tarsiers can often be good attackers and main part of your strategy
[2018-04-16 16:49] darkeisbein: and as said before the best attackers are often located in the random set cards
[2018-04-16 16:53] masn6811 (Masne98888): common mistake i see with people is building animus for tarsier when they already have 3 forges
[2018-04-16 16:54] masn6811 (Masne98888): sure, tarsier is more efficient than splitter, but only by a small amount
[2018-04-16 16:54] masn6811 (Masne98888): not enough to make up for floating the blue in most cases
[2018-04-16 16:56] darkeisbein: if you are already floating blue/green just spam goose/splitters
